title: A Solo RPG with an entire galaxy to explore (Space Aces - review and gameplay)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m6233lu5J9Q

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to one of his favourite solo RPG zines with Space Aces: Voyages in Infinite Space, the expanded follow-up to Space Aces: The New Guidebook — a Saturday-morning-cartoon sci-fi universe he pitches as Crusher Joe and Cowboy Bebop by way of Star Blazers. He's candid about his first reaction: disappointment. The new version strips out the original's stats entirely — no more Moxie, Smarts, or Wiggles — leaving characters with just a career, a knack, a quest, and gumption. But playing it won him over, and the video shows why: the escalating heat level that snowballs dramatic tension, the push-your-luck delve system for exploring space hulks (demonstrated in a genuinely tense treasure hunt on Starbase 42 that goes down to zero supply and busts), and the Galaxy in a Box tiles, each star sector backed by pages of adventures, delves, and creatures. The extended play example is the highlight: rolling up lifeguard-turned-adventurer Sam Foyle and his crew, losing a scavenger-hunt race for quantum-entangled doorways, getting shaken down twice by the same holographically camouflaged pirates, and crash-landing on a planet mid-jousting-tournament. Daniel also shares a house-rule usage-die system for simulating wealth, and closes with pairing suggestions including the Star Dogs referee handbook.

----

Daniel initially bounced off the stripped-down character sheet before embracing the game for what it is. When has a radically simplified second edition won you over — or lost you for good?
